title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot visits Mrs. Oliver (Ariadne) at her home, where he is introduced to her friend, Judith Butler. Judith, a woman with golden hair and sea-green eyes, met Ariadne in Greece after pulling her from the sea. During tea, served by Ariadne's daughter, Miranda, the conversation turns to the murder of Joyce Reynolds. Mrs. Butler questions why Joyce, who was known for lying, would suddenly claim to have seen a murder, but Poirot confirms that Joyce was indeed murdered.

The group discusses biblical stories from the Apocrypha, specifically the tale of Judith and Holofernes and the story of Jael, who killed Sisera by driving a nail into his head. Miranda expresses her distaste for such violence, stating that if she had to deal with enemies, she would prefer to use a drug to allow them to die peacefully in their sleep. 

After Miranda leaves to wash the dishes, Mrs. Oliver expresses her anxiety regarding the local atmosphere and the safety of children, urging Poirot to find the killer so the community can feel at ease again. Poirot and Mrs. Oliver move into the garden to sit on a stone bench among the Elizabethan queen roses. There, Mrs. Oliver asks Poirot for his impression of Judith.
